message: Add scaffold for add and remove

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+---
+title: add
+category: List
+example: "xs.add(y)"
+---
+
+Inserts an element into a list if it doesn't already exist (set-insertion).
+
+```tql
+add(xs:list, x:any) -> list
+```
+
+## Description
+
+The `add` function returns the list `xs` with `x` inserted at the end, but only
+if `x` is not already present in the list. This performs a set-insertion
+operation, ensuring no duplicate values in the resulting list.
+
+## Examples
+
+### Add a new element to a list
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(4)
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 2, 3, 4]}
+```
+
+### Try to add an existing element
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(2)
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+```
+
+### Add to an empty list
+
+```tql
+from {xs: []}
+xs = xs.add("hello")
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: ["hello"]}
+```
+
+## See Also
+
+[`append`](/reference/functions/append),
+[`prepend`](/reference/functions/prepend),
+[`remove`](/reference/functions/remove)

@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+---
+title: remove
+category: List
+example: "xs.remove(y)"
+---
+
+Removes all occurrences of an element from a list.
+
+```tql
+remove(xs:list, x:any) -> list
+```
+
+## Description
+
+The `remove` function returns the list `xs` with all occurrences of `x` removed.
+If `x` is not present in the list, the original list is returned unchanged.
+
+## Examples
+
+### Remove an element from a list
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 2, 4]}
+xs = xs.remove(2)
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 3, 4]}
+```
+
+### Remove a non-existent element
+
+```tql
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.remove(5)
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+```
+
+### Remove from a list with strings
+
+```tql
+from {xs: ["apple", "banana", "apple", "orange"]}
+xs = xs.remove("apple")
+```
+
+```tql
+{xs: ["banana", "orange"]}
+```
+
+## See Also
+
+[`add`](/reference/functions/add),
+[`append`](/reference/functions/append),
+[`prepend`](/reference/functions/prepend)

@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+// test: exec
+
+// Test basic add functionality
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(4)
+
+// Test adding existing element
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(2)
+
+// Test adding to empty list
+from {xs: []}
+xs = xs.add(42)
+
+// Test with strings
+from {xs: ["apple", "banana"]}
+xs = xs.add("orange")
+
+// Test with existing string
+from {xs: ["apple", "banana"]}
+xs = xs.add("apple")
+
+// Test with mixed types
+from {xs: [1, "two", 3]}
+xs = xs.add(4)
+
+// Test with null values
+from {xs: [1, null, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(null)
+
+// Test with null list
+from {xs: null}
+xs = xs.add(1)
+
+// Test multiple operations
+from {xs: [1, 2]}
+xs = xs.add(3).add(2).add(4)
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+// test: exec
+
+// Test basic remove functionality
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 4]}
+xs = xs.remove(2)
+
+// Test removing non-existent element
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.remove(5)
+
+// Test removing all occurrences
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 2, 4, 2]}
+xs = xs.remove(2)
+
+// Test removing from empty list
+from {xs: []}
+xs = xs.remove(1)
+
+// Test with strings
+from {xs: ["apple", "banana", "apple", "orange"]}
+xs = xs.remove("apple")
+
+// Test removing non-existent string
+from {xs: ["apple", "banana"]}
+xs = xs.remove("cherry")
+
+// Test with mixed types
+from {xs: [1, "two", 3, "two"]}
+xs = xs.remove("two")
+
+// Test with null values
+from {xs: [1, null, 3, null]}
+xs = xs.remove(null)
+
+// Test with null list
+from {xs: null}
+xs = xs.remove(1)
+
+// Test multiple operations
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]}
+xs = xs.remove(2).remove(4)
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+# Test add function with various scenarios
+
+# Basic add operations
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(4)
+---
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.add(2)
+---
+from {xs: []}
+xs = xs.add(1)
+---
+
+# Add with null lists
+from {xs: null}
+xs = xs.add(1)
+---
+
+# Add null element to list
+from {xs: [1, 2]}
+xs = xs.add(null)
+---
+
+# Add to list with mixed types
+from {xs: [1, "hello", true]}
+xs = xs.add(3.14)
+---
+from {xs: [1, "hello", true]}
+xs = xs.add("hello")
+---
+
+# Add with nested lists
+from {xs: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]}
+xs = xs.add([5, 6])
+---
+from {xs: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]}
+xs = xs.add([1, 2])
+---
+
+# Add records to list
+from {xs: [{a: 1}, {b: 2}]}
+xs = xs.add({c: 3})
+---
+from {xs: [{a: 1}, {b: 2}]}
+xs = xs.add({a: 1})
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+# Test remove function with various scenarios
+
+# Basic remove operations
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3, 2]}
+xs = xs.remove(2)
+---
+from {xs: [1, 2, 3]}
+xs = xs.remove(4)
+---
+from {xs: [1]}
+xs = xs.remove(1)
+---
+
+# Remove with null lists
+from {xs: null}
+xs = xs.remove(1)
+---
+
+# Remove null element from list
+from {xs: [1, null, 2, null]}
+xs = xs.remove(null)
+---
+
+# Remove from list with mixed types
+from {xs: [1, "hello", true, "hello"]}
+xs = xs.remove("hello")
+---
+from {xs: [1, "hello", true]}
+xs = xs.remove(42)
+---
+
+# Remove with nested lists
+from {xs: [[1, 2], [3, 4], [1, 2]]}
+xs = xs.remove([1, 2])
+---
+from {xs: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]}
+xs = xs.remove([5, 6])
+---
+
+# Remove records from list
+from {xs: [{a: 1}, {b: 2}, {a: 1}]}
+xs = xs.remove({a: 1})
+---
+from {xs: [{a: 1}, {b: 2}]}
+xs = xs.remove({c: 3})
+---
+
+# Remove all occurrences
+from {xs: [1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1]}
+xs = xs.remove(1)
\ No newline at end of file
